page: Microsoft Azure E48ds_v7 is an Edsv7-family virtual machine configured with 48 vCPUs and 384.0 GB of RAM, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It is powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor operating at 4.2 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, featuring 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and 8-bit integer operations.

This server includes 2835 GB of local storage and is capable of utilizing Premium Storage. In memory benchmarks, the system achieves a read-only cached small-block memory bandwidth of 3532.63 GB/sec, while large-block uncached read and write bandwidths reach 162.68 GB/sec and 66.17 GB/sec respectively. These metrics place its memory performance in the average tier.

Computational evaluations show strong performance in specific workloads, with a PassMark CPU score of 64146.76 and a single-threaded gzip compression rate of 35.53 GB/sec, placing it in the top 10% of tested servers. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression reaches 272.18 GB/sec, categorized as strong. However, the system exhibits a clear tradeoff in large-scale machine learning tasks; while prompt processing for a 7B LLM model is strong at 214.51 tokens/sec, text generation for a 70B model drops to a weak 2.64 tokens/sec due to the lack of dedicated GPU hardware.

With its high memory density and local storage capacity, the E48ds_v7 offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads that do not require GPU acceleration. It is suited for relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis—where it processes 76.28 million SET operations per second—and static web serving at 61.11 GB/sec.
description: Microsoft Azure E48ds_v7 is an Edsv7-family virtual machine featuring 48 vCPUs and 384.0 GB of RAM, powered by a dedicated 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 24 physical cores. It includes 2835 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. The architecture features a 480 MB L3 cache and supports AVX-512 and Intel AMX instructions. Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities in database operations, with Redis SET operations reaching 76.28 million per second, and a top-tier single-threaded gzip compression speed of 35.53 GB/sec. However, without a GPU, it exhibits weak performance in large-scale LLM text generation, making it better suited for memory-optimized workloads, in-memory caching, static web serving, and relational databases rather than heavy AI model execution.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E48ds_v7 is an Edsv7 virtual machine with 48 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 2835 GB local storage. It features a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU and delivers strong database and memory-optimized performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E48ds_v7 is a memory-optimized VM featuring 48 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, 2835 GB local storage, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing, substantial local storage, and advanced matrix extensions for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 48 vCPUs and 384 GB RAM providing a 1:8 memory-to-vCPU ratio
Dedicated 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with AMX and AVX-512
2835 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong database performance with 76.28 million Redis SET operations per second
Top-tier single-threaded gzip compression at 35.53 GB/sec
Suited for in-memory caching, relational databases, and static web serving
